body:
Title: CRA Form T2203 2025 Ontario and Manitoba multiple-jurisdiction tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2203/t2203-25e.pdf

CRA T2203 2025 Form ON428MJ calculates Ontario tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.0505, 0.0915, 0.1116, 0.1216, 0.1316, 0.2463, 0.20, 0.36, 0.25, and 0.06. ON428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $52,886, $105,775, $150,000, and $220,000; rates 5.05%, 9.15%, 11.16%, 12.16%, and 13.16%; and base amounts $0.00, $2,670.74, $7,510.09, $12,445.60, and $20,957.60. Lines 9 to 13 subtract Ontario non-refundable tax credits and multiply by the Ontario allocation percentage. Lines 14 to 21 apply the resident adjustment based on lines 58330 and 58360 at 5.05% and add Ontario unallocated tax on split income. Lines 22 to 29 subtract the prorated Ontario minimum tax carryover at 24.63%. Lines 31 to 41 calculate Ontario surtax using thresholds $5,710 and $7,307, rates 20% and 36%, and the Ontario allocation percentage. Lines 42 to 46 subtract the Ontario dividend tax credit for residents and add additional tax for minimum tax purposes. Lines 47 to 58 calculate Ontario tax reduction using a $294 basic reduction and $544 amounts for dependent children and dependants with impairment. Lines 60 to 67 subtract Ontario foreign tax credit, prorated LIFT credit from Schedule ON428-A MJ, and the 25% community food program donation credit for farmers. Worksheet ON428MJ line 68 calculates the Ontario health premium using taxable income thresholds $20,000, $25,000, $36,000, $38,500, $48,000, $48,600, $72,000, $72,600, $200,000, and $200,600, rates 6% and 25%, and fixed premiums $300, $450, $600, $750, and $900. Line 69 enters Ontario tax on Part 5 line 5.

CRA T2203 2025 Form MB428MJ calculates Manitoba tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.108, 0.1275, 0.174, 0.50, 0.75, and 0.3333. MB428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $47,000 and $100,000; rates 10.8%, 12.75%, and 17.4%; and base amounts $0.00, $5,076.00, and $11,833.50. Lines 9 to 16 add Manitoba unallocated tax on split income and subtract non-refundable tax credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover at 50%. Lines 17 to 20 add additional tax for minimum tax purposes at 50% and multiply by the Manitoba allocation percentage. For residents, lines 21 to 28 subtract the resident adjustment based on lines 58315, 58316, 58325, 58326, 58330, and 58360 at 10.8%, the Manitoba dividend tax credit, and the percentage of income not allocated to Manitoba. Lines 30 to 31 calculate the Manitoba political contribution tax credit using contribution thresholds $400 and $750, rates 75%, 50%, and 33.33%, base credits $300 and $475, and a $1,000 maximum. Lines 32 to 44 subtract labour-sponsored funds credit, provincial foreign tax credit, community enterprise development tax credit, small business venture capital tax credit capped at $120,000, employee share purchase tax credit, and mineral exploration tax credit, then enter Manitoba tax on Part 5 line 6.
